project.objective: Identify the parents of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Ireland, immigrated to Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, died 1908

questions.question: Who were the parents of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, Ireland, d. 1908 PA)?
questions.rationale: This is the primary research objective.

items.rationale: A Flynn marriage shortly before Patrick's ~1845 birth identifies the couple and supplies the mother's maiden name.
Patrick's own Catholic baptism would name his parents directly — the decisive child-linking record.
A US census placing Patrick as a child in a parents' household would link him to them.

log.notes: Found the marriage of Thomas Flynn and Mary Doyle, 12 Feb 1843, County Mayo — both fathers named. Predates Patrick's ~1845 birth, so the couple existed as a unit before he was born.

No baptism for a Patrick Flynn ca. 1845 in the Mayo parishes searched. The register for the relevant years is patchy; nothing naming Patrick's parents was found. This is the decisive child-linking record and it was not recovered.

Patrick does not appear as a child in any US household enumeration — his earliest US record is as an adult householder (1870). He immigrated after childhood, so no census places him in his parents' household.

sources.citation: Marriage of Thomas Flynn and Mary Doyle, 12 February 1843, Roman Catholic parish, County Mayo, Ireland; National Library of Ireland Catholic Parish Registers, indexed on FamilySearch, accessed 2 May 2026.
sources.notes: Index entry; original register image not examined.

assertions.value: Thomas Flynn married Mary Doyle, 12 Feb 1843, County Mayo
assertions.informant: Officiating priest recording the marriage at the time of the event
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Parish priest recorded the marriage contemporaneously; direct evidence of the marriage event itself.

person_evidence.rationale: The groom Thomas Flynn in the 1843 marriage matches the candidate father stub I2 by name and Mayo origin. This establishes that Thomas married Mary Doyle before Patrick's birth — it does NOT by itself place Patrick as their child.
The bride Mary Doyle in the same marriage matches the candidate mother stub I3 and supplies the maiden name. Like pe_001, it links the couple to each other, not Patrick to the couple.
